title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning  Service Account Related Roles to Users

input_row.title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning  Service Account Related Roles to Users
input_row.description: It is recommended that the principle of 'Separation of Duties' is enforced while assigning service-account related roles to users.
input_row.rationale: The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account admin allows the user/identity to create, delete, and manage service account(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account User allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on Compute and App Engine) to assign service account(s) to Apps/Compute Instances. Separation of duties is the concept of ensuring that one individual does not have all necessary permissions to be able to complete a malicious action. In Cloud IAM - service accounts, this could be an action such as using a service account to access resources that user should not normally have access to. Separation of duties is a business control typically used in larger organizations, meant to help avoid security or privacy incidents and errors. It is considered best practice. No user should have Service Account Admin and Service Account User roles assigned at the same time.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. Ensure no member has the roles Service Account Admin and Service account User assigned together. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all users and role assignments: gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format json | \ jq -r '[ (["Service_Account_Admin_and_User"] | (., map(length*"-"))), ( [ .bindings[] | select(.role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin" or .role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser").members[] ] | group_by(.) | map({User: ., Count: length}) | .[] | select(.Count == 2).User | unique ) ] | .[] | @tsv' 2. All common users listed under Service_Account_Admin_and_User are assigned both the roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin and roles/iam.serviceAccountUser roles.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. For any member having both Service Account Admin and Service account User roles granted/assigned, click the Delete Bin icon to remove either role from the member. Removal of a role should be done based on the business requirements.
input_row.impact: The removed role should be assigned to a different user based on business needs.
input_row.additional_information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Service Account Admin and Service Account User. To avoid the misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to very limited users and Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal. These requirements are addressed in separate recommendations.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) or GCP CLI. The audit specifies a detailed command structure that can retrieve IAM policies and check for specific roles assigned to users. Additionally, there is no requirement for SSH/RDP access or any manual review beyond what's specified in the CLI commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_service_account_roles_separation_of_duties

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- Utilize the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) with the command:
  ```
  gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format json
  ```
  This command retrieves the IAM policy for the specified project in JSON format.

- The retrieved IAM policy should be processed to identify users who have both the `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` roles assigned. This can be achieved by parsing the JSON output to group and count the assigned roles for each user in the bindings.

- Expected configuration properties to validate:
  - Users with the combination of both roles should not exist.
  
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - The output should find no users listed in a combination of both roles. If any user is returned as having both roles, the control fails.
  
- Example validation could include:
  - Validate the count of users assigned to roles:
    ```json
    {
      "bindings": [
        {
          "role": "roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin",
          "members": [
            "user:exampleuser@example.com"
          ]
        },
        {
          "role": "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser",
          "members": [
            "user:exampleuser@example.com"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
    ```

In this example, `exampleuser@example.com` is a failing case as they are assigned both roles, which violates the separation of duties principle.
